title: #eng-runtime: 1776451055
preview:
Talia Benson: need to lock canary plan today — which regions first + who’s on point for watch/rollback. goal: enable new kernel paths behind flags for a small cohort before we touch broader hosted traffic.

Rafael Mendes: from SRE side: prefer a region w/ good observability + easy rollback + minimal enterprise sensitivity. I’d start w/ us-west (lowest enterprise SLAs impact for our current cohorts) then us-east. I’d avoid eu-west as first step given last week’s noise + that SUP ticket.

Grace Kim: +1 avoid eu-west first. the TTFT regression we saw was in a subset of prompts but it’ll be hard to debug w/ EU variance/driver skew.

Allison Grant: dashboards can slice by region/model/kernel-mode now (post pr-19934), so any region is “watchable”. but agree eu-west is historically noisier due to mixed fleet images.

Hiro Tanaka: for canary cohort do we want FP8-only first, or FP8+INT8 together? I’m worried about conflating issues.

Sofia Ivanova: scheduling knobs are part of the patch too (batch window/backpressure defaults). if we roll both kernels + scheduler at once, attribution is messy. but splitting rollouts increases time.

Rafael Mendes: recommendation: canary w/ kernel-mode changes first (FP8 attention + INT8 matmul dispatch) with scheduler knobs still at baseline, then enable scheduler tuning after we confirm kernel doesn’t regress TTFT. we can do 2-phase within same region.

Yuki Sato: feasible. flags we have:
- `runtime.kernel.fp8_attention_enabled`
- `runtime.kernel.int8_matmul_enabled`
- `runtime.dispatch.autoselect_heuristics_v2`
- `runtime.scheduler.mixed_workload_defaults_v1`
We can gate by model+region in perf-canary config.

Connor O'Brien: release-wise that maps cleanly to RC notes too. we can communicate as “phase 1 kernels, phase 2 scheduler”.

Talia Benson: ok. for “regions first” sounds like us-west -> us-east -> eu-central? (eu-west last).

Irene Choi: +1 on us-west first. fleet there is cleanest for H100 pool + least MIG weirdness. us-east is bigger but has a couple older driver pockets; we can pin the canary cohort to the known-good nodepool.

Allison Grant: metrics to watch (primary):
- TTFT p50/p95
- TPOT p50/p95
- tokens/s (decode throughput)
- error rate (5xx + model errors)
Secondary:
- scheduler queue depth histos
- GPU SM util + HBM bw
- GPU mem OOM/evictions
I can drop links once we agree the region list.

Rafael Mendes: rollback triggers: propose automated rollback in perf-canary if ANY of:
1) p95 TTFT regression > 4% vs baseline for 20 min (min 500 reqs)
2) p95 TPOT regression > 3% for 20 min (min 500 reqs)
3) error rate > 0.25% for 10 min
4) OOM/eviction counters > 2x baseline
Noise guard: require both A/A baseline and canary have enough samples; otherwise alert only (no auto rollback) and page human.

Grace Kim: can we tighten TTFT regression threshold for the prefill-heavy workloads? the FP8 attention path mostly affects prefill. p95 TTFT is the one that will hurt customer perception.

Rafael Mendes: cautious about too tight given run-to-run variance. we can add a “hard” threshold (4%) and a “soft” threshold (2%) that pages but doesn’t auto rollback.

Allison Grant: yep, I can configure:
- WARN: >2% for 15m (page #eng-runtime-oncall + #eng-sre)
- ROLLBACK: >4% for 20m with sample floor
Also tagging by `model`, `gpu_sku`, `kernel_mode` is already in place so we can spot if it’s one model only.

Hiro Tanaka: cohorting by model: I’d start with 2 models max. pick one “easy” (short context, common) + one “stress” (longer context / bigger hidden size). we’ve seen shape-specific issues.

Sofia Ivanova: agree but pick models that represent the mixed workload traffic; otherwise scheduler interactions will surprise us later.

Talia Benson: what are the proposed canary models?

Grace Kim: for hosted top traffic: Model A (short prompts, high QPS) + Model B (longer prompts). don’t want to name them here, but the “top-6 hosted models” list from ENG-7428 has the exact IDs.

Yuki Sato: we can implement as:
- us-west: enable `autoselect_heuristics_v2` + fp8/int8 flags for Model A/B only, 1% of traffic (or a dedicated canary cohort key)
- after 2 hrs stable, increase to 5%
- then us-east same plan

Neha Kapoor: pls keep us-west canary to a nodepool that’s NOT shared w/ Dedicated burst capacity. we got burned last sprint w/ contention (SUP-31867).

Irene Choi: +1. I can reserve a small H100 slice in us-west for canary specifically (separate from benchmark reservation). will post details in #eng-infra.

Rafael Mendes: ownership: SRE will run the enable/rollback. need runtime person to sit with us for first 4 hrs.

Hiro Tanaka: I can cover first window. if it slips, Grace can cover.

Grace Kim: ok.

Allison Grant: Grafana links:
- Canary overview: https://grafana.redwood.internal/d/fp8-int8-canary/overview
- Breakdown: https://grafana.redwood.internal/d/fp8-int8-canary/by-model
- Scheduler queue depth: https://grafana.redwood.internal/d/runtime-scheduler/queue-depth
(If these 404 for anyone, you might need the perf-canary role binding.)

Connor O'Brien: comms template? we should pre-stage the message in #eng-runtime and #eng-sre when flipping.

Talia Benson: I’ll draft:
- when starting us-west 1% canary
- when ramping 5%
- if rollback triggered
- when moving to us-east

PerfCanaryBot: (configured) Profile `fp8_int8_v1` updated: WARN ttft_p95=+2%/15m, ROLLBACK ttft_p95=+4%/20m, WARN tpot_p95=+2%/15m, ROLLBACK tpot_p95=+3%/20m, ROLLBACK error_rate=0.25%/10m. Sample floor: 500 requests.

Rafael Mendes: nice. last question: eu-central vs ap-southeast next? I’d do eu-central after us-east, then ap-southeast once we confirm driver parity.

Irene Choi: eu-central is cleaner than eu-west for fleet consistency. ap-southeast still has a mixed driver pocket; I’d rather not until we finish image pinning.

Talia Benson: decision:
1) Canary order: us-west -> us-east -> eu-central -> eu-west (last)
2) Phase 1: kernels + autoselect heuristics for Model A/B only
3) Phase 2: scheduler defaults
4) Cohort: 1% -> 5% (hold 2h min between ramps)
Rafael/Hiro pair for first window. Allison owns dashboards/alerts tuning.

Hiro Tanaka: :thumbsup:

Rafael Mendes: :thumbsup: will open the change in our oncall tracker + link runbook (perf-canary rollout doc).

Allison Grant: I’ll watch alert noise; if we see small-sample false positives, we’ll switch the WARN to page-only + keep rollback strict on sample floor.

Neha Kapoor: thanks — keep me posted if you need more reserved capacity once you move beyond 5%.

Talia Benson: cool. I’ll paste this into the sprint daily notes + update ENG-7431 checklist.
text:
Talia Benson: need to lock canary plan today — which regions first + who’s on point for watch/rollback. goal: enable new kernel paths behind flags for a small cohort before we touch broader hosted traffic.

Rafael Mendes: from SRE side: prefer a region w/ good observability + easy rollback + minimal enterprise sensitivity. I’d start w/ us-west (lowest enterprise SLAs impact for our current cohorts) then us-east. I’d avoid eu-west as first step given last week’s noise + that SUP ticket.

Grace Kim: +1 avoid eu-west first. the TTFT regression we saw was in a subset of prompts but it’ll be hard to debug w/ EU variance/driver skew.

Allison Grant: dashboards can slice by region/model/kernel-mode now (post pr-19934), so any region is “watchable”. but agree eu-west is historically noisier due to mixed fleet images.

Hiro Tanaka: for canary cohort do we want FP8-only first, or FP8+INT8 together? I’m worried about conflating issues.

Sofia Ivanova: scheduling knobs are part of the patch too (batch window/backpressure defaults). if we roll both kernels + scheduler at once, attribution is messy. but splitting rollouts increases time.

Rafael Mendes: recommendation: canary w/ kernel-mode changes first (FP8 attention + INT8 matmul dispatch) with scheduler knobs still at baseline, then enable scheduler tuning after we confirm kernel doesn’t regress TTFT. we can do 2-phase within same region.

Yuki Sato: feasible. flags we have:
- `runtime.kernel.fp8_attention_enabled`
- `runtime.kernel.int8_matmul_enabled`
- `runtime.dispatch.autoselect_heuristics_v2`
- `runtime.scheduler.mixed_workload_defaults_v1`
We can gate by model+region in perf-canary config.

Connor O'Brien: release-wise that maps cleanly to RC notes too. we can communicate as “phase 1 kernels, phase 2 scheduler”.

Talia Benson: ok. for “regions first” sounds like us-west -> us-east -> eu-central? (eu-west last).

Irene Choi: +1 on us-west first. fleet there is cleanest for H100 pool + least MIG weirdness. us-east is bigger but has a couple older driver pockets; we can pin the canary cohort to the known-good nodepool.

Allison Grant: metrics to watch (primary):
- TTFT p50/p95
- TPOT p50/p95
- tokens/s (decode throughput)
- error rate (5xx + model errors)
Secondary:
- scheduler queue depth histos
- GPU SM util + HBM bw
- GPU mem OOM/evictions
I can drop links once we agree the region list.

Rafael Mendes: rollback triggers: propose automated rollback in perf-canary if ANY of:
1) p95 TTFT regression > 4% vs baseline for 20 min (min 500 reqs)
2) p95 TPOT regression > 3% for 20 min (min 500 reqs)
3) error rate > 0.25% for 10 min
4) OOM/eviction counters > 2x baseline
Noise guard: require both A/A baseline and canary have enough samples; otherwise alert only (no auto rollback) and page human.

Grace Kim: can we tighten TTFT regression threshold for the prefill-heavy workloads? the FP8 attention path mostly affects prefill. p95 TTFT is the one that will hurt customer perception.

Rafael Mendes: cautious about too tight given run-to-run variance. we can add a “hard” threshold (4%) and a “soft” threshold (2%) that pages but doesn’t auto rollback.

Allison Grant: yep, I can configure:
- WARN: >2% for 15m (page #eng-runtime-oncall + #eng-sre)
- ROLLBACK: >4% for 20m with sample floor
Also tagging by `model`, `gpu_sku`, `kernel_mode` is already in place so we can spot if it’s one model only.

Hiro Tanaka: cohorting by model: I’d start with 2 models max. pick one “easy” (short context, common) + one “stress” (longer context / bigger hidden size). we’ve seen shape-specific issues.

Sofia Ivanova: agree but pick models that represent the mixed workload traffic; otherwise scheduler interactions will surprise us later.

Talia Benson: what are the proposed canary models?

Grace Kim: for hosted top traffic: Model A (short prompts, high QPS) + Model B (longer prompts). don’t want to name them here, but the “top-6 hosted models” list from ENG-7428 has the exact IDs.

Yuki Sato: we can implement as:
- us-west: enable `autoselect_heuristics_v2` + fp8/int8 flags for Model A/B only, 1% of traffic (or a dedicated canary cohort key)
- after 2 hrs stable, increase to 5%
- then us-east same plan

Neha Kapoor: pls keep us-west canary to a nodepool that’s NOT shared w/ Dedicated burst capacity. we got burned last sprint w/ contention (SUP-31867).

Irene Choi: +1. I can reserve a small H100 slice in us-west for canary specifically (separate from benchmark reservation). will post details in #eng-infra.

Rafael Mendes: ownership: SRE will run the enable/rollback. need runtime person to sit with us for first 4 hrs.

Hiro Tanaka: I can cover first window. if it slips, Grace can cover.

Grace Kim: ok.

Allison Grant: Grafana links:
- Canary overview: https://grafana.redwood.internal/d/fp8-int8-canary/overview
- Breakdown: https://grafana.redwood.internal/d/fp8-int8-canary/by-model
- Scheduler queue depth: https://grafana.redwood.internal/d/runtime-scheduler/queue-depth
(If these 404 for anyone, you might need the perf-canary role binding.)

Talia Benson: I’ll draft:

- if rollback triggered
- when moving to us-east

Irene Choi: eu-central is cleaner than eu-west for fleet consistency. ap-southeast still has a mixed driver pocket; I’d rather not until we finish image pinning.

title: #eng-runtime: 1776442209
preview:
Talia Benson: daily matrix check-in — pls drop status + gaps for H100/A100 (and L4 if anyone got cycles). goal today: close remaining top-6 hosted models @ baseline + FP8 + INT8 where supported.

Talia Benson: benchmark matrix sheet (v1): https://drive.google.com/.../fp8-int8-benchmark-matrix-v1 (tabs per GPU). please mark rows green/yellow/red + owner.

Hiro Tanaka: thx. reminder: if a case is blocked by correctness, mark as RED + link to thread/issue. don’t keep re-running hoping it goes away :)

Grace Kim: H100 tab update:
- Model A (70B) ✅ baseline + FP8 ✅ + INT8 ✅ (all workloads: prefill-heavy, decode-heavy, mixed)
- Model B (8x7B) ✅ baseline ✅ FP8 ✅ INT8 ⚠️ (INT8 fails on long-context decode shape, guardrail kicks to baseline but runner marks as “mixed”; need to split / record as fallback)
- Model C (34B) ✅ baseline ✅ FP8 ✅ INT8 ✅
Remaining on H100: Model D (tool-calling variant) mixed workload still yellow — waiting on scheduler metrics tags from pr-19855 build.

Rina Kobayashi: ^ on the INT8 “mixed” status: runner currently reports kernel-mode = requested, not effective (after fallback). we should annotate those rows manually until pr-19934 lands everywhere.

Marcus Reed: scheduler metrics tagging is in runtime build `1.22.0-rc3+19855` (sha `d4c1b7e`). if your cluster is still on rc2 you won’t see prefill/decode split histograms.

Isabella Rossi: A100 tab:
- Model A ✅ baseline ✅ FP8 ✅ INT8 ✅ (but variance high on mixed — p95 swings ~8-10% run-to-run)
- Model B ✅ baseline ✅ FP8 ✅ INT8 ⚠️ (INT8 matmul path disabled for one shape regime; expected fallback. marking as “INT8=fallback” in notes)
- Model C ✅ baseline ✅ FP8 ✅ INT8 ✅
Gaps: Model D + Model E still missing FP8 on A100 in us-east (runner jobs keep landing on nodes with driver 535, need 550 per pinned image).

Irene Choi: re driver mismatch: we found one pool in us-east `rt-a100-bench-02` still on the old AMI. i can drain + reprovision but it’ll take ~2h. alternatively route jobs to `rt-a100-bench-01` (already updated).

Vanessa Ortiz: +1 to routing. also pls avoid the dedicated-a100-prod pool even if it looks empty (saw some folks try yesterday). :no_entry:

Omar El-Sayed: scheduler side: I ran mixed workload for Model A + Model C on H100 with batching_window=2ms vs 6ms.
- 2ms improves TTFT but loses some throughput, net tokens/s down ~4-6%
- 6ms realizes more of FP8 decode win, but TTFT p95 gets spiky under concurrency=32.
I’ll add a note in the “scheduler-impact” doc + link rows.

Lena Fischer: for the remaining mixed cases, can someone confirm we’re using the same concurrency ladder? I’m seeing some runs at {8,16,32} and others at {4,8,16}.

Rina Kobayashi: spec is {8,16,32} for hosted realism. {4,8,16} was from the calibration template. if you have older artifacts, keep them but don’t aggregate into the main sheet.

Allison Grant: observability note: if you want dashboard slices later, please ensure artifacts include `model_id`, `gpu_sku`, `kernel_mode_requested`, and ideally `kernel_mode_effective`. missing tags = I can’t build clean canary comparisons.

Yuki Sato: heuristics implementation check: FP8 attention should only be selected for head_dim=128 on H100; on A100 we expect baseline attention for long context. if you’re seeing FP8 attention on A100 long-context, pls paste logs (could be stale registry).

Grace Kim: I saw one A100 long-context attempt pick fp8-attn v2 unexpectedly. logs show `registry_rev=118` (old). likely cluster still on older container. I’ll rerun after Irene’s pool fix.

Julian Moore: for folks blocked on “scheduler metrics”: you can sanity check build has the right counters by hitting `/metrics` and searching:
`runtime_scheduler_prefill_ms_bucket`
`runtime_scheduler_decode_ms_bucket`
`runtime_scheduler_queue_depth_bucket`
If absent, you’re not on pr-19855.

Rafael Mendes: SRE ask: please don’t run matrix jobs in eu-west today 16:00-20:00 UTC (canary window). keep benchmark load in us-east/us-central unless pre-approved.

Talia Benson: consolidating gaps from above:
1) A100 us-east driver mismatch -> Irene draining `rt-a100-bench-02` (ETA ~2h). Isabella/Grace rerun Model D/E FP8 after.
2) INT8 “requested vs effective” confusion -> annotate fallback rows manually; longer term pr-19934.
3) Missing H100 Model D tool-calling mixed workload -> needs rc3 build w/ scheduler tags. owner?

Marcus Reed: I can take H100 Model D tool-calling mixed on `rt-h100-bench-03` after lunch. will use rc3 + lock clocks.

Lena Fischer: I can own Model E mixed on A100 once the pool is fixed. will stick to concurrency {8,16,32} and gen_len=256 per suite v3.

Isabella Rossi: for variance on A100 mixed: I suspect clocks weren’t locked (I forgot `--lock-gpu-clocks`). re-running 3x with locks + warmup=200 iters.

Grace Kim: +1 variance — also please make sure no debug logging. I saw `RUNTIME_LOG_LEVEL=debug` in one env dump. that’ll wreck p95.

Rina Kobayashi: omg yes. pls use the runbook template env. (also pr-19918 removes the noisy logging path but not everywhere yet)

Hiro Tanaka: nice. once the remaining reds are resolved, I want a quick summary per GPU: “coverage % + known exclusions + reason”. makes ADR + release notes much easier.

Talia Benson: action items (today):
- Marcus: H100 Model D tool-calling mixed (rc3), upload artifacts + mark sheet
- Grace/Isabella: rerun A100 Model D/E FP8 after pool fix
- Lena: A100 Model E mixed (standard ladder)
- Everyone: annotate INT8 fallback rows clearly (“effective=baseline”) + link any correctness thread

Talia Benson: next check-in in ~5h in-thread. :thumbsup:

Irene Choi: update: drain started on `rt-a100-bench-02`. new image is `bench-a100-550.54-cuda12.4`.

Isabella Rossi: ack. I’ll queue reruns to `rt-a100-bench-01` now to avoid waiting, then backfill once `-02` is clean.

Rafael Mendes: thx. also reminder: keep artifact uploads out of the canary bucket today — use `bench-artifacts/dev/` not `bench-artifacts/canary/`.

Allison Grant: if anyone captures nsys traces for the variance case, drop link in #eng-platform too. we can correlate with GPU util dips.

Talia Benson: noted. closing loop later today.

title: #eng-runtime: 1776419988
preview:
Grace Kim: seeing a TTFT regression in a subset of prompts w/ FP8 attention enabled (flag `runtime.kernels.fp8_attention=true`). Not everywhere, but enough to fail the nightly perf gate on H100.

Grace Kim: quick stats from matrix runner (top-hosted-suite-v3, H100, model=“mistral-7b-instruct”):
- baseline TTFT p50: ~235ms, p95: ~410ms
- FP8-attn TTFT p50: ~245ms (+4%), p95: ~515ms (+25%)
TPOT looks basically flat / slightly better, so it’s mostly prefill.

Grace Kim: the regressing cases are mostly short prompts + tool/structured variant (few hundred tokens prefill), smaller batch sizes (b<=4). Hypothesis: kernel launch overhead / scheduler mix causing prefill fragmentation.

Hiro Tanaka: :eyes: thanks. do we have the breakdown by prefill vs decode time from the new scheduler metrics? (PR-19855)

Julian Moore: yeah, I can pull from the `scheduler_prefill_ms` histogram. For the failing cohort, prefill wall time is up but GPU active time isn’t up proportionally.

Julian Moore: that usually screams “more launches / less batching” or CPU-side overhead.

Sofia Ivanova: +1. if FP8 attention path changes how we chunk prefill (or creates smaller work units), scheduler interleaving can get worse. esp if decode traffic is present and we’re in mixed mode.

Omar El-Sayed: do we know if these runs are mixed prefill/decode or pure prefill microbench?

Isabella Rossi: these are end-to-end serving benchmarks (matrix runner), mixed workload profile “prod-ish”: prefill 60% / decode 40%, concurrency=16.

Isabella Rossi: microbench for attention alone shows FP8 winning across most shapes, so this seems like an e2e scheduling interaction.

Noah Patel: do we tag metrics by kernel mode now? if not, it’s hard to confirm we’re actually dispatching FP8 attention only for those requests.

Julian Moore: we do in the perf suite logs but not in the serving metrics unless you’re on the build with `metrics_tagging_model_gpu_kernel_mode` (PR-19934). The matrix runner artifacts include the kernel registry dump though.

Grace Kim: confirmed dispatch is FP8 attention for those cases (from artifact `kernel_registry.json` + request trace). It flips only attention; matmuls still baseline.

Hiro Tanaka: can you paste 1-2 exemplar prompt configs that regress? want to see seq lens.

Grace Kim: examples (from suite):
- `tool_calling_short_03`: prompt_len ~ 280 tokens, gen_len 64, batch mostly 1-2
- `structured_json_short_07`: prompt_len ~ 410 tokens, gen_len 32
Both hit TTFT p95 spikes.

Sofia Ivanova: that’s the annoying regime where you’re latency sensitive + launch overhead dominates. FP8 kernel might have higher fixed overhead even if better at scale.

Rina Kobayashi: also check if we’re accidentally enabling extra syncs around the FP8 path (alignment check / workspace alloc). there was a similar issue last quarter.

Isabella Rossi: I can repro locally on a single H100 with fixed clocks and a stripped harness. If it reproduces, I’ll grab nsys.

Hiro Tanaka: please grab nsys for baseline vs FP8 on one of those prompts. we need CPU launch timeline + kernel counts.

Grace Kim: +1, and can we capture CUPTI counters? want to see if we’re underutilizing SMs in the regressing case.

Allison Grant: FYI the Grafana panel `perf-canary / TTFT (p95) by prompt_variant` is lighting up only on those two variants. Link: https://grafana.prod.redwood/… (internal)

Rafael Mendes: from SRE side: no customer impact yet (this is sprint canary only), but if we can’t bound it we should keep FP8 attention behind the per-model flag and exclude short-prompt variants.

Noah Patel: we can add a heuristic: enable FP8 attention only when (prefill_tokens * batch) >= threshold, otherwise baseline. But we need data to pick the threshold.

Omar El-Sayed: scheduler knob angle: if the regression is from prefill fragmentation, a slightly larger batching window for short-prefill requests could fix it. But we need to be careful not to hurt TTFT elsewhere.

Sofia Ivanova: agreed. maybe do two experiments:
1) fixed scheduler, compare kernels
2) fixed kernel, sweep batching window/backpressure
so we can attribute.

Grace Kim: I’ll queue two reruns:
- FP8 attention ON, scheduler defaults
- FP8 attention ON, `batching_window_ms=1.5` (current is 0.7) + keep decode priority same
and compare TTFT/queue depth.

Julian Moore: I’ll add extra logging for “prefill chunk count” per request in the harness (not in prod) so we can correlate chunking with TTFT spikes.

Rina Kobayashi: also sanity: ensure debug logging isn’t on in those runs. we had a regression once because someone enabled verbose kernel registry prints.

Isabella Rossi: debug logs are off (`RUNTIME_LOG_LEVEL=info`). But I’ll confirm there isn’t an fp8 path doing extra `cudaDeviceSynchronize` for error checking.

Hiro Tanaka: there is an `#ifdef DEBUG_FP8_VALIDATE` in the integration branch; should be off, but double-check build flags.

Grace Kim: ack. I’ll check the container build metadata in the artifact (git sha + cmake flags).

Allison Grant: perf canary threshold currently is -0% / +10% on TTFT p95 for these variants. We’re breaching +20-30% so it’s a legit rollback trigger if this hits broader canary.

Rafael Mendes: action items before we even think about expanding canary:
- identify if regression is fixed overhead vs scheduling
- add a guardrail heuristic or shape exclusion
- document rollback criteria in the runbook

Noah Patel: proposal: temporary mitigation = disable FP8 attention when prompt_len < 512 AND batch <= 2. That matches observed set. We can refine after profiling.

Sofia Ivanova: I’m ok with a conservative guardrail as long as it’s explicit + measured. But let’s not bake in magic numbers without at least one nsys proof.

Hiro Tanaka: +1. wait for traces.

Isabella Rossi: I’ll post nsys links here tonight. If it’s launch count, we’ll know fast.

Grace Kim: thanks all. I’ll update once reruns finish + share deltas. :thumbsup:

(PerfBot): Perf suite alert: `top-hosted-suite-v3` H100 `mistral-7b-instruct` FP8-attn TTFT p95 regression detected (+24.7%). Run: `matrix_2026-04-17_02-13Z` Artifact: s3://redwood-perf/artifacts/… Reaction: :warning:

Grace Kim: :facepalm: ok at least it’s consistent.

Julian Moore: quick note: queue depth p95 is also higher in the regressing cohort (prefill queue). points again at scheduler interaction.

Sofia Ivanova: cool, that’s a good signal. once we have traces we can decide whether to fix in kernel (reduce launch overhead) or in scheduler (batching/coalescing).
text:
Grace Kim: seeing a TTFT regression in a subset of prompts w/ FP8 attention enabled (flag `runtime.kernels.fp8_attention=true`). Not everywhere, but enough to fail the nightly perf gate on H100.

Grace Kim: quick stats from matrix runner (top-hosted-suite-v3, H100, model=“mistral-7b-instruct”):
- baseline TTFT p50: ~235ms, p95: ~410ms
- FP8-attn TTFT p50: ~245ms (+4%), p95: ~515ms (+25%)
TPOT looks basically flat / slightly better, so it’s mostly prefill.

Grace Kim: the regressing cases are mostly short prompts + tool/structured variant (few hundred tokens prefill), smaller batch sizes (b<=4). Hypothesis: kernel launch overhead / scheduler mix causing prefill fragmentation.

Hiro Tanaka: :eyes: thanks. do we have the breakdown by prefill vs decode time from the new scheduler metrics? (PR-19855)

Julian Moore: yeah, I can pull from the `scheduler_prefill_ms` histogram. For the failing cohort, prefill wall time is up but GPU active time isn’t up proportionally.

Julian Moore: that usually screams “more launches / less batching” or CPU-side overhead.

Sofia Ivanova: +1. if FP8 attention path changes how we chunk prefill (or creates smaller work units), scheduler interleaving can get worse. esp if decode traffic is present and we’re in mixed mode.

Omar El-Sayed: do we know if these runs are mixed prefill/decode or pure prefill microbench?

Isabella Rossi: these are end-to-end serving benchmarks (matrix runner), mixed workload profile “prod-ish”: prefill 60% / decode 40%, concurrency=16.

Isabella Rossi: microbench for attention alone shows FP8 winning across most shapes, so this seems like an e2e scheduling interaction.

Noah Patel: do we tag metrics by kernel mode now? if not, it’s hard to confirm we’re actually dispatching FP8 attention only for those requests.

Julian Moore: we do in the perf suite logs but not in the serving metrics unless you’re on the build with `metrics_tagging_model_gpu_kernel_mode` (PR-19934). The matrix runner artifacts include the kernel registry dump though.

Grace Kim: confirmed dispatch is FP8 attention for those cases (from artifact `kernel_registry.json` + request trace). It flips only attention; matmuls still baseline.

Hiro Tanaka: can you paste 1-2 exemplar prompt configs that regress? want to see seq lens.

Grace Kim: examples (from suite):
- `tool_calling_short_03`: prompt_len ~ 280 tokens, gen_len 64, batch mostly 1-2
- `structured_json_short_07`: prompt_len ~ 410 tokens, gen_len 32
Both hit TTFT p95 spikes.

Sofia Ivanova: that’s the annoying regime where you’re latency sensitive + launch overhead dominates. FP8 kernel might have higher fixed overhead even if better at scale.

Rina Kobayashi: also check if we’re accidentally enabling extra syncs around the FP8 path (alignment check / workspace alloc). there was a similar issue last quarter.

Isabella Rossi: I can repro locally on a single H100 with fixed clocks and a stripped harness. If it reproduces, I’ll grab nsys.

Hiro Tanaka: please grab nsys for baseline vs FP8 on one of those prompts. we need CPU launch timeline + kernel counts.

Grace Kim: +1, and can we capture CUPTI counters? want to see if we’re underutilizing SMs in the regressing case.

Allison Grant: FYI the Grafana panel `perf-canary / TTFT (p95) by prompt_variant` is lighting up only on those two variants. Link: https://grafana.prod.redwood/… (internal)

Rafael Mendes: from SRE side: no customer impact yet (this is sprint canary only), but if we can’t bound it we should keep FP8 attention behind the per-model flag and exclude short-prompt variants.

Noah Patel: we can add a heuristic: enable FP8 attention only when (prefill_tokens * batch) >= threshold, otherwise baseline. But we need data to pick the threshold.

Omar El-Sayed: scheduler knob angle: if the regression is from prefill fragmentation, a slightly larger batching window for short-prefill requests could fix it. But we need to be careful not to hurt TTFT elsewhere.

Sofia Ivanova: agreed. maybe do two experiments:
1) fixed scheduler, compare kernels
2) fixed kernel, sweep batching window/backpressure
so we can attribute.

Grace Kim: I’ll queue two reruns:
- FP8 attention ON, scheduler defaults
- FP8 attention ON, `batching_window_ms=1.5` (current is 0.7) + keep decode priority same
and compare TTFT/queue depth.

Julian Moore: I’ll add extra logging for “prefill chunk count” per request in the harness (not in prod) so we can correlate chunking with TTFT spikes.

Rina Kobayashi: also sanity: ensure debug logging isn’t on in those runs. we had a regression once because someone enabled verbose kernel registry prints.

Isabella Rossi: debug logs are off (`RUNTIME_LOG_LEVEL=info`). But I’ll confirm there isn’t an fp8 path doing extra `cudaDeviceSynchronize` for error checking.

Hiro Tanaka: there is an `#ifdef DEBUG_FP8_VALIDATE` in the integration branch; should be off, but double-check build flags.

Grace Kim: ack. I’ll check the container build metadata in the artifact (git sha + cmake flags).

Allison Grant: perf canary threshold currently is -0% / +10% on TTFT p95 for these variants. We’re breaching +20-30% so it’s a legit rollback trigger if this hits broader canary.

Rafael Mendes: action items before we even think about expanding canary:
- identify if regression is fixed overhead vs scheduling
- add a guardrail heuristic or shape exclusion
- document rollback criteria in the runbook

Noah Patel: proposal: temporary mitigation = disable FP8 attention when prompt_len < 512 AND batch <= 2. That matches observed set. We can refine after profiling.

Sofia Ivanova: I’m ok with a conservative guardrail as long as it’s explicit + measured. But let’s not bake in magic numbers without at least one nsys proof.

Hiro Tanaka: +1. wait for traces.

Isabella Rossi: I’ll post nsys links here tonight. If it’s launch count, we’ll know fast.

Grace Kim: thanks all. I’ll update once reruns finish + share deltas. :thumbsup:

(PerfBot): Perf suite alert: `top-hosted-suite-v3` H100 `mistral-7b-instruct` FP8-attn TTFT p95 regression detected (+24.7%). Run: `matrix_2026-04-17_02-13Z` Artifact: s3://redwood-perf/artifacts/… Reaction: :warning:

Grace Kim: :facepalm: ok at least it’s consistent.

Julian Moore: quick note: queue depth p95 is also higher in the regressing cohort (prefill queue). points again at scheduler interaction.

Sofia Ivanova: cool, that’s a good signal. once we have traces we can decide whether to fix in kernel (reduce launch overhead) or in scheduler (batching/coalescing).

title: #eng-runtime: 1776401123
preview:
Talia Benson: Kicking off the FP8/INT8 kernel benchmarking sprint thread. Goal: defensible + reproducible perf matrix (microbench + e2e serving) and translate into runtime decisions (autoselect + scheduler knobs + quant profiles) w/ safe rollout via flags + perf canary.

Talia Benson: Sprint plan (WIP but has the skeleton + owners): https://redwoodinference.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENGSRV/pages/FP8-INT8-Kernel-Benchmarking-Sprint-Plan
Linear epic: https://linear.app/redwood/issue/ENG-7421/fp8-int8-kernel-benchmarking-sprint-epic

Talia Benson: Proposed initial in-scope matrix (please react / comment):
GPUs: H100 (SXM), A100 (80GB), + 1 cost SKU (L4 if available in benchmark lab)
Models (top hosted, v1 list):
- llama-3.1-70b-instruct
- mixtral-8x7b-instruct
- qwen2.5-72b-instruct
- gemma-2-27b-it
- llama-3.1-8b-instruct
- mistral-small (our default “mid”)
Workloads: suite v3 (prefill-heavy, decode-heavy, mixed; tool calling + structured output variant)

Talia Benson: Owners draft:
- Kernel sprint lead: @Hiro Tanaka
- Scheduling sprint lead: @Sofia Ivanova
- Harness owner / runner: @Rina Kobayashi
- FP8/INT8 bench execution: @Grace Kim + @Isabella Rossi
- Autoselect impl: @Yuki Sato
- Evals / signoff: @Jonas Weber + @Claire Dubois
- Canary/rollout: @Rafael Mendes + @Allison Grant

Talia Benson: If you’re on point for anything above, please confirm here + drop any hard constraints (PTO, travel, etc). Also: if you think the model list is off, reply with suggested swaps + why (traffic share / archetype coverage). :spiral_calendar_pad:

Hiro Tanaka: Confirmed on kernel lead. :thumbsup: 
From kernel side: we should explicitly separate (a) matmul INT8 paths (GEMM-heavy) vs (b) FP8 attention kernels (shapes + head_dim sensitivity). The harness needs to tag kernel mode by component or we’ll be guessing.

Grace Kim: +1. I can own FP8 attention comparisons + keep a running “good shapes / bad shapes” notes doc. We’ll need to lock down the prefill/decode splits early so we don’t churn.

Sofia Ivanova: Confirmed scheduling lead. We need scheduler instrumentation in place before we interpret any end-to-end wins, otherwise we’ll just be measuring queueing. @Julian Moore can you confirm bandwidth to land the prefill/decode breakdown + queue depth metrics this week?

Julian Moore: Yep, I can prioritize ENG-7425. I have a branch already with `prefill_ms`, `decode_ms`, and a queue depth histogram tagged by route/model. I’ll post a PR link once CI is green.

Rina Kobayashi: Harness owner confirmed. Before we run the full matrix: I want sign-off on methodology: warmup rules, clock pinning, driver/CUDA pinning, and minimum N runs. We have variance issues on H100 if clocks aren’t locked (saw this last month).

Claire Dubois: +1 on methodology rigor. Please make sure we’re capturing full env metadata in artifacts (driver, CUDA, container digest, GPU clocks, MIG/partition info). If we can’t reproduce within ~1–2% we can’t use these numbers anywhere.

Isabella Rossi: I can help with microbench harness + shape sweeps (matmul + attention). Suggest we allocate day 2 for calibration runs only (no “real results”) to shake out debug logging / weird cache effects.

Talia Benson: Noted. I’ll update sprint plan to explicitly call out “calibration day” + methodology sign-off as a gate before we open the floodgates.

Irene Choi: On capacity: I can reserve H100 + A100 in benchmark-lab clusters, but I need the expected hours + regions by EOD. Also please confirm we’re not touching any Dedicated pools.

Neha Kapoor: +1 to Irene. We can approve spend but need a clean reservation plan + guardrails (no surprise contention). If L4 capacity is limited, we can do a smaller subset just to get directional data.

Grace Kim: For hours: rough cut: H100 ~250 GPU-hours, A100 ~180 GPU-hours, L4 ~60 GPU-hours (subset models only). This assumes ~6 models * ~5 workloads * ~3 modes (baseline/FP8/INT8) with repeats + some retries.

Rina Kobayashi: That’s plausible but depends on how we handle tool-calling variants (some prompts produce longer decode). I’ll add a “time estimate” section once suite v3 is finalized.

Jonas Weber: Evals/signoff: confirmed. Please don’t enable defaults based purely on perf. We need quick sanity evals at minimum for any model where quantization changes output distribution (we’ve seen long-context brittleness).

Jonas Weber: For sprint scope, propose: eval “gates” only for models we intend to flip default (hosted catalog). For everything else: collect perf + quality notes, but keep behind flags.

Henry Cho: From Perf transparency side: we’ll need the benchmark matrix to include footnotes (exact GPU SKU, driver/CUDA, workload description, variance) if we want to re-use any excerpt externally later. Happy to review the “what we can claim” section once we have a draft.

Aditya Rao: +1, and please tag results in a way Optimize can consume (FP8 eligible? INT8 eligible? conditions). Even a simple per-model “recommended modes” table w/ caveats would unblock.

Omar El-Sayed: On workload representativeness: can we confirm the prefill/decode distribution matches production? The batch/concurrency regime matters more than people think. If we’re using v3 suite, please share the assumptions (prompt len dist, gen len dist, concurrency).

Talia Benson: Good call. Suite v3 definition is here (draft): https://redwoodinference.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/AML/pages/Top-Hosted-Models-Benchmark-Suite-v3
I’ll ask Applied ML to confirm traffic mix assumptions + “top hosted models” list signoff in #eng-ml too.

Yuki Sato: Autoselect implementation: confirmed. Please keep a crisp output from the sprint: “if GPU=H100 and head_dim=128 and seq_len regime=long => choose FP8 attention v2 else fallback baseline” etc. I can wire the heuristics into dispatch, but I need the decision table (and guardrails) by end of week 1.

Hiro Tanaka: On model list: looks ok, but I’d swap “mistral-small” for “deepseek-r1-distill-llama-70b” only if it’s actually top traffic. Otherwise keep a smaller model to capture memory-bound vs compute-bound regimes.

Talia Benson: Let’s keep mistral-small for now unless we have data that distill is top-6 hosted. I’ll pull the traffic list from analytics and post here.

Allison Grant: For dashboards: if we can tag metrics by `model`, `gpu_sku`, `kernel_mode`, and `region`, we can build canary views quickly. Please ensure ENG-7427 includes those dimensions (and that they’re stable strings).

Rafael Mendes: Canary owner confirmed. Please don’t underestimate noise at small sample sizes. We’ll want thresholds by SKU + per-model baselines, and a rollback policy that’s conservative but not flappy.

Connor O'Brien: Release coordination: I’ll track this under runtime 1.22.0 release train. Need: flag names + default states + “how to rollback” steps before we cut RC.

Rina Kobayashi: Action items I’m capturing:
1) Talia: finalize model list (top hosted) + update sprint plan w/ calibration day + deliverable checklist
2) Rina: methodology doc draft + harness changes list + artifact schema
3) Irene/Neha: capacity reservation plan pending GPU-hour estimates + time windows
4) Julian: land scheduler instrumentation PR
5) Jonas/Claire: define eval signoff criteria + which prompt sets are required

Talia Benson: Perfect. Please add any missing action items + owners. I’ll post a short “kickoff summary” back into the sprint plan page and keep this thread as the running coordination spot. :white_check_mark:

title: #eng-runtime: 1771396404
preview:
Noah Patel: Kicking off eu-west expansion for perf-canary. Goal: get canary running + baselines aligned before we flip alerts on. 

Proposed steps:
1) deploy canary in eu-west in observe-only (no warn/page)
2) run 24–48h synthetic workload, validate metric parity w/ us-east
3) baseline gen job writes eu-west artifacts (SKU-aware)
4) compare baseline deltas + variance, decide thresholds
5) enable warning alerts, then paging after 1 week if clean

Need owners to confirm: model list, SKU, and dashboard links. :eyes:

Vanessa Ortiz: eu-west fleet is mixed right now: mostly H100-80GB, but there’s still a small A100-80GB pool on Dedicated. For hosted it’s H100 only. If canary fans out across both, baselines will be messy.

Noah Patel: +1. Let’s pin canary targets to hosted tier first (H100). Dedicated expansion later once we’ve got region+SKU thresholds.

Sofia Ivanova: Agree. Also need to validate scheduler queueing metrics in eu-west. We saw queue_time behave weirdly when telemetry lagged.

Logan Wright: Telemetry lag in eu-west should be better than it was 2 weeks ago (we upgraded the collector). Still: can we add a quick “ingestion delay” panel to the dashboard so oncall doesn’t confuse late metrics for regressions?

Allison Grant: Yes please. Paging hygiene: any new region should have a "telemetry healthy" gate before alerts. Otherwise we repeat the alert storm.

Nadia Rahman: On baselines: if we’re doing region+SKU artifacts, I need confirmation on the key. Currently baseline store key is:
`{model_id}:{model_version}:{runtime_build}:{sku}`
(no region). 

If we add region, it becomes:
`{model_id}:{model_version}:{runtime_build}:{region}:{sku}`
This avoids overfitting but increases artifact count.

Noah Patel: For eu-west rollout, I’d rather include region. We already saw eu-west has different p95 from us-east due to network + fleet tuning.

Omar El-Sayed: +1 region. Runtime-only latency should be close, but e2e includes queueing + some control-plane overhead that differs. If we don’t include region, the evaluator will constantly scream.

Rina Kobayashi: On workload: do we keep the same prompt-shape mix as us-east? Or adjust for eu-west traffic (shorter prompts on average, I think)?

Noah Patel: Keep same v0 distribution for now. We need comparability. Once stable, we can add a eu-west-specific profile, but that’s v2.

Vanessa Ortiz: Confirming: hosted eu-west is `h100-80gb-sxm` only. Dedicated is `a100-80gb` and some `h100-80gb-pcie`. If canary config is tier-scoped, we can stay in hosted.

Noah Patel: Great. Proposed model list (same as phase 1 us-east):
- llama-3.1-70b-instruct
- qwen2.5-72b-instruct
- mistral-large-2

Can we confirm these are actually enabled + healthy in eu-west hosted?

Connor O'Brien: From releases side: eu-west has llama + qwen. mistral-large-2 is enabled but on older runtime build due to driver constraint last month. Might matter for baseline alignment.

Sofia Ivanova: That’s actually good to catch. If runtime_build differs, baseline should be per runtime_build anyway, but we need to ensure canary is hitting the same build as customer traffic.

Omar El-Sayed: Can we log runtime_build + kernel tag for eu-west runs in the canary logs? (sanity check) We added tags but I don’t trust config drift across regions.

Logan Wright: Metrics already include `runtime_build` + `kernel_id` labels. Please don’t add more labels. Cardinality is borderline as-is. For sanity check, use logs (structured field) or a low-cardinality gauge.

Noah Patel: Ack. We’ll add a once-per-minute log line in canary with:
`region, target_tier, model_id, model_version, runtime_build (observed), kernel_id (observed), sku`
No new metric labels.

Allison Grant: Also want alert payload to include region explicitly. Otherwise incident-bot threads are confusing.

Rafael Mendes: +1. On-call needs to know "eu-west only" vs global. Canary alerts should route to #eng-runtime but paging should go to oncall only after we’ve got 7d of clean warns.

Noah Patel: Plan:
- Week 0: observe-only (no alerts)
- Week 1: warnings enabled (non-paging)
- Week 2: paging enabled for top 2 models only if SRE signs off

Sound ok?

Rafael Mendes: Works. I’ll want a runbook blurb: "if eu-west only, check fleet change + region deployment history".

Nadia Rahman: Baseline gen: I can run the baseline job in eu-west once canary is deployed and emitting. Need confirm bucket/path + IAM already in place for eu-west.

Connor O'Brien: IAM should already cover region, but the baseline job service account may not have access to the eu-west artifact prefix. I’ll check with infra.

deploybot: Deploy requested by Noah Patel: `perf-canary` -> environment `prod`, region `eu-west`, mode `observe_only`, tier `hosted`. (Change ID: RC-88421)

Sofia Ivanova: Before we deploy: can we double-check QPS caps in eu-west? Don’t want to eat capacity during peak EU hours.

Noah Patel: Cap is still 0.2 QPS per model, plus budget enforcement. Should be <0.3% GPU per region for these 3 models.

Vanessa Ortiz: Peak is 08:00–11:00 UTC. If you can avoid turning it on right at peak that’d be great.

Noah Patel: Deploy now but canary starts with a 30m warmup at 10% QPS, then ramps. Should be fine.

Omar El-Sayed: Please verify queueing_time metrics in eu-west show non-zero under load. In us-east we saw zeros when scheduler sampling was off.

Logan Wright: I can add a quick query snippet here for validation:

`sum(rate(redwood_runtime_queueing_time_ms_bucket{region="eu-west",le="+Inf"}[5m])) by (model_id)`

If that’s flat zero while requests are >0, something’s wrong.

grafanabot: Dashboard updated: `Perf Canary / Per-Model Overview` now includes region selector and "telemetry ingestion delay" panel. https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/perf-canary-overview (commit 9f3c2b)

Allison Grant: :thumbsup: please ensure that link works without being logged into the prod VPN (oncall often on mobile). If not, add the "open in console" fallback.

Noah Patel: Good call. I’ll ping Chloe separately for console deep links.

---

Noah Patel: Update: deploy succeeded. Canary running in eu-west observe-only. Seeing metrics for llama + qwen, mistral is returning 404 from target endpoint.

Vanessa Ortiz: 404 likely means mistral-large-2 not enabled in eu-west hosted (it might be Dedicated-only there). Connor’s note about older runtime build was for Dedicated.

Connor O'Brien: Confirmed: mistral-large-2 is NOT enabled in eu-west hosted. It’s only in a dedicated pool for one customer.

Noah Patel: Ok, we’ll drop mistral from eu-west hosted canary for now. Replace with `mixtral-8x22b-instruct`? Is that enabled in eu-west hosted?

Connor O'Brien: mixtral is enabled in eu-west hosted.

Rina Kobayashi: mixtral has higher variance on long prompts. Might require wider thresholds. But fine if we start warnings only.

Nadia Rahman: I’m seeing baseline evaluator complaining because baseline artifacts for eu-west don’t exist yet (expected). It’s currently falling back to us-east baseline key because region isn’t part of key.

Noah Patel: Right, we need to land the region-in-key change before we go further. Nadia can you own? I’ll review quickly.

Nadia Rahman: Yep. I’ll update baseline store key + backfill logic. For safety: when region is missing, evaluator should NOT fallback across regions. It should go "no baseline" -> observe-only.

Allison Grant: +1000 please. Cross-region fallback is how we get nonsense paging.

Logan Wright: If you change keys, remember to update dashboard queries that reference baseline artifact labels (if any). Keep it backwards compatible for at least a week.

Nadia Rahman: Will do. I’ll add dual-read: prefer region+sku, else region-less, but only if region == us-east (for backwards compat). For eu-west, it’ll require region key.

Noah Patel: Works.

---

Noah Patel: After 6h, early stats look reasonable:
- llama p95 e2e is ~8–12% higher vs us-east
- runtime-only p95 is within ~3%
- queueing_time p95 is higher during 09:00 UTC (EU peak)

That suggests infra/traffic shape differences, not kernel.

Sofia Ivanova: Queueing spike lines up with smaller batch sizes? If batch_size distribution shifts, tokens/sec will drop even if runtime is stable.

Logan Wright: Panel shows batch_size p50 in eu-west is 6 vs 9 in us-east during peak. That’s likely scheduler config / traffic mix.

Vanessa Ortiz: eu-west has fewer concurrent tenants per GPU (capacity isolation), so batching is naturally lower. Baselines should reflect that.

Noah Patel: Great. This is exactly why region baselines.

Rafael Mendes: When do we enable warning alerts? Want at least one full weekday cycle + weekend cycle.

Noah Patel: Suggest: keep observe-only until we have 48h baseline + confirm no telemetry gaps. Then warnings.

Allison Grant: And add a "telemetry gap" check: if ingestion delay > X or scrape failures, suppress warnings. 

Noah Patel: Logged. We can use the ingestion-delay gauge as a condition in alert rules.

---

Connor O'Brien: FYI infra confirmed baseline job SA now has access to `s3://perf-baselines/eu-west/*`.

Nadia Rahman: Baseline gen job running now for eu-west hosted H100. ETA 30 min for first artifacts.

Noah Patel: :pray: once artifacts are in, I’ll flip evaluator to use region key and confirm no "missing baseline" spam.

Sofia Ivanova: Please post the baseline diff summary when ready. I want to sanity-check prefill vs decode deltas.

Nadia Rahman: Will do. Also, I’m planning initial thresholds for eu-west to be slightly wider on e2e p95 (15%) but keep runtime-only tight (7%). We can tune after warnings.

Allison Grant: That aligns with policy. Runtime-only signal should page earlier than e2e (less noisy), but only if we trust instrumentation.

Omar El-Sayed: We do trust it now. Queueing vs execution split in eu-west looks correct.

Noah Patel: Cool. Next checkpoint tomorrow: if baselines stable + no gaps, enable warning alerts (eu-west only) for llama + qwen + mixtral.

Rafael Mendes: Works. Please make sure warning alerts don’t fan out to oncall pager yet. Route to #eng-runtime only.

Noah Patel: Yep. Will keep `severity=warn` + channel routing only.

Allison Grant: Add note in alert description: "Region: eu-west (phase 1), do not trigger rollback unless sustained + corroborated by customer metrics".

Noah Patel: Done. I’ll update the alert template once Nadia’s key change is merged.
text:
Noah Patel: Kicking off eu-west expansion for perf-canary. Goal: get canary running + baselines aligned before we flip alerts on. 

Proposed steps:
1) deploy canary in eu-west in observe-only (no warn/page)
2) run 24–48h synthetic workload, validate metric parity w/ us-east
3) baseline gen job writes eu-west artifacts (SKU-aware)
4) compare baseline deltas + variance, decide thresholds
5) enable warning alerts, then paging after 1 week if clean

Need owners to confirm: model list, SKU, and dashboard links. :eyes:

Vanessa Ortiz: eu-west fleet is mixed right now: mostly H100-80GB, but there’s still a small A100-80GB pool on Dedicated. For hosted it’s H100 only. If canary fans out across both, baselines will be messy.

Noah Patel: +1. Let’s pin canary targets to hosted tier first (H100). Dedicated expansion later once we’ve got region+SKU thresholds.

Sofia Ivanova: Agree. Also need to validate scheduler queueing metrics in eu-west. We saw queue_time behave weirdly when telemetry lagged.

Logan Wright: Telemetry lag in eu-west should be better than it was 2 weeks ago (we upgraded the collector). Still: can we add a quick “ingestion delay” panel to the dashboard so oncall doesn’t confuse late metrics for regressions?

Allison Grant: Yes please. Paging hygiene: any new region should have a "telemetry healthy" gate before alerts. Otherwise we repeat the alert storm.

Nadia Rahman: On baselines: if we’re doing region+SKU artifacts, I need confirmation on the key. Currently baseline store key is:
`{model_id}:{model_version}:{runtime_build}:{sku}`
(no region). 

If we add region, it becomes:
`{model_id}:{model_version}:{runtime_build}:{region}:{sku}`
This avoids overfitting but increases artifact count.

Noah Patel: For eu-west rollout, I’d rather include region. We already saw eu-west has different p95 from us-east due to network + fleet tuning.

Omar El-Sayed: +1 region. Runtime-only latency should be close, but e2e includes queueing + some control-plane overhead that differs. If we don’t include region, the evaluator will constantly scream.

Rina Kobayashi: On workload: do we keep the same prompt-shape mix as us-east? Or adjust for eu-west traffic (shorter prompts on average, I think)?

Noah Patel: Keep same v0 distribution for now. We need comparability. Once stable, we can add a eu-west-specific profile, but that’s v2.

Vanessa Ortiz: Confirming: hosted eu-west is `h100-80gb-sxm` only. Dedicated is `a100-80gb` and some `h100-80gb-pcie`. If canary config is tier-scoped, we can stay in hosted.

Noah Patel: Great. Proposed model list (same as phase 1 us-east):
- llama-3.1-70b-instruct
- qwen2.5-72b-instruct
- mistral-large-2

Can we confirm these are actually enabled + healthy in eu-west hosted?

Connor O'Brien: From releases side: eu-west has llama + qwen. mistral-large-2 is enabled but on older runtime build due to driver constraint last month. Might matter for baseline alignment.

Sofia Ivanova: That’s actually good to catch. If runtime_build differs, baseline should be per runtime_build anyway, but we need to ensure canary is hitting the same build as customer traffic.

Omar El-Sayed: Can we log runtime_build + kernel tag for eu-west runs in the canary logs? (sanity check) We added tags but I don’t trust config drift across regions.

Logan Wright: Metrics already include `runtime_build` + `kernel_id` labels. Please don’t add more labels. Cardinality is borderline as-is. For sanity check, use logs (structured field) or a low-cardinality gauge.

Noah Patel: Ack. We’ll add a once-per-minute log line in canary with:
`region, target_tier, model_id, model_version, runtime_build (observed), kernel_id (observed), sku`
No new metric labels.

Allison Grant: Also want alert payload to include region explicitly. Otherwise incident-bot threads are confusing.

Rafael Mendes: +1. On-call needs to know "eu-west only" vs global. Canary alerts should route to #eng-runtime but paging should go to oncall only after we’ve got 7d of clean warns.

Noah Patel: Plan:
- Week 0: observe-only (no alerts)
- Week 1: warnings enabled (non-paging)
- Week 2: paging enabled for top 2 models only if SRE signs off

Sound ok?

Rafael Mendes: Works. I’ll want a runbook blurb: "if eu-west only, check fleet change + region deployment history".

Nadia Rahman: Baseline gen: I can run the baseline job in eu-west once canary is deployed and emitting. Need confirm bucket/path + IAM already in place for eu-west.

Connor O'Brien: IAM should already cover region, but the baseline job service account may not have access to the eu-west artifact prefix. I’ll check with infra.

deploybot: Deploy requested by Noah Patel: `perf-canary` -> environment `prod`, region `eu-west`, mode `observe_only`, tier `hosted`. (Change ID: RC-88421)

Sofia Ivanova: Before we deploy: can we double-check QPS caps in eu-west? Don’t want to eat capacity during peak EU hours.

Noah Patel: Cap is still 0.2 QPS per model, plus budget enforcement. Should be <0.3% GPU per region for these 3 models.

Vanessa Ortiz: Peak is 08:00–11:00 UTC. If you can avoid turning it on right at peak that’d be great.

Noah Patel: Deploy now but canary starts with a 30m warmup at 10% QPS, then ramps. Should be fine.

Omar El-Sayed: Please verify queueing_time metrics in eu-west show non-zero under load. In us-east we saw zeros when scheduler sampling was off.

Logan Wright: I can add a quick query snippet here for validation:

`sum(rate(redwood_runtime_queueing_time_ms_bucket{region="eu-west",le="+Inf"}[5m])) by (model_id)`

If that’s flat zero while requests are >0, something’s wrong.

grafanabot: Dashboard updated: `Perf Canary / Per-Model Overview` now includes region selector and "telemetry ingestion delay" panel. https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/perf-canary-overview (commit 9f3c2b)

Allison Grant: :thumbsup: please ensure that link works without being logged into the prod VPN (oncall often on mobile). If not, add the "open in console" fallback.

Noah Patel: Good call. I’ll ping Chloe separately for console deep links.

---

title: #eng-runtime: 1771234567
preview:
Omar Rahman: Heads up — after the kernel-selection change that prefers the new FP4-sparse matmul for short sequences, we saw a ~25% throughput drop and intermittent memory spikes on prod-gpu-pool-3 around 06:42 UTC. graph: https://grafana.internal/r/xyz :eyes:
Priya Desai: ugh. do we have profiler traces? is this isolated to NF4 vs FP4 or the kernel switch path?
Omar Rahman: I grabbed a quick pprof on one affected node. attaching snippet below. high memcpy counts and sudden allocations in kv_cache codepath.
Alice Chen: paste of profiler (truncated):
```
CPU: 0.8s total
memcpy: 320ms
kernel_fp4_sparse_matmul: 180ms
kv_cache_flush: 90ms
alloc: 250MB spikes at request start
```
Noticed frequent kv_cache->evict cycles at ~1.2s intervals when sequence length > 512.
Mateo Alvarez: that eviction pattern smells like batching heuristic holding onto KV pages until a full batch triggers, then we do a big memcpy to populate sparse kernel input format. FP4-sparse needs data layout transform which seems to be O(n) and allocating a temp buffer per request.
Sofia Li: infra-bot showed a platform rollout for the kernel selector at 06:30 UTC (deploy 8c9a7). infra-bot: "deploy: kernel-selector v2 -> rollout 10% -> prod-gpu-pool-3"
Priya Desai: can you share the exact reproducer command? i'll run a controlled sweep (seq len, batch size, kv hit rate)
Omar Rahman: repro linked — run on canary: 
```
./bench_infer --model rwd-llm-13b-nf4 --quant nf4-fp4-sparse --seq 1024 --concurrency 8 --kv=on --prefix_cache=enabled --tokens 20000
```
Note: switching quant to nf4-int8 removes the spike.
Alice Chen: dug into the kernel impl — when sparse kernel path chosen we call convert_to_sparse_layout() which allocates a temp tensor sized [batch, heads, seq, head_dim_quant] per request and doesn't reuse across requests. Previously dense kernels used a shared scratch allocator. That's the mem leak-ish behavior.
Mateo Alvarez: so two issues: 1) layout transformation allocation pattern (per-request scratch) and 2) batching heuristic causes accumulation of larger seq groups that hit the conversion more often. Both increase memcpy + alloc time.
Priya Desai: hypothesis: calibration step for FP4 quantizer emits per-tensor scale tables which force extra per-layer metadata and a small fallback path that uses host-side staging buffers. That combined with layout conversion increases host<->device transfers. Do we have traces for PCIe memcpy vs device memcpy?
Omar Rahman: yes — host memcpy dominated. device memcpy negligible. That aligns with staging buffers on host.
Sofia Li: immediate mitigation options: rollback kernel-selector rollout OR patch kernel to use shared scratch pool and enable fallback to int8 for seq>512. Which is lower risk?
Mateo Alvarez: patching kernel for scratch reuse is low-risk but needs careful testing (sync/parallelism). Fallback to int8 for long seq is safer and fast to flip via policy.
Priya Desai: agree — enable policy: if seq_len>512 or kv_cache_hit_rate>0.6, route to int8 kernel. Also add a flag to disable FP4-sparse layout transform temporary allocation. I'll open a small PR.
Omar Rahman: canary plan: Sofia — can you flip policy on prod-canary for 1 hour with traffic mirror and run repro? i'll watch p99 and allocation metrics.
Sofia Li: on it. will schedule at 07:15 UTC.
Alice Chen: i'll prototype scratch-pool patch in a branch (alice/fp4-scratch-pool) and add unit tests for concurrent requests. Might take 2 days.
Omar Rahman: action items summarized:
- Sofia: flip policy to fallback int8 for long seq on prod-canary at 07:15 UTC (Sofia)
- Priya: PR for routing policy + tests (Priya)
- Alice: implement shared scratch allocator + tests (Alice)
- Mateo: add batching heuristic telemetry to detect accumulation patterns (Mateo)
I'll own the rollout rollback if canary still spikes.
Priya Desai: FYI i'll also run a calibration sanity check — maybe our FP4 calibrator produced extreme scale values for some layers after last model import.
Omar Rahman: thanks all. please post short updates in thread. :thumbsup:

title: #eng-runtime: 1770974409
preview:
Noah Patel: want to propose we add a couple attribution metrics to perf-canary + runtime telemetry. right now when we see p95 up or tok/s down we’re still guessing if it’s prefill vs decode vs scheduler vs batching.

Noah Patel: specifically:
- prefill vs decode time split (runtime-only)
- batch depth / effective batch size during decode
- maybe “tokens per request” + “prefill tokens” so we can normalize shapes

Sofia Ivanova: +1. queueing_time helps, but last incident we couldn’t tell if tok/s drop was due to smaller batches or slower kernel.

Sofia Ivanova: for batching depth, can we expose something like: avg_active_sequences, avg_batch_size, and distribution? tail matters.

Omar El-Sayed: we have some of this internally in scheduler debug counters, but not exported in a stable way.

Omar El-Sayed: what’s the minimum set you want on the canary side vs globally in runtime metrics?

Noah Patel: for canary RCA I’d take:
1) `runtime_prefill_ms` and `runtime_decode_ms` (per request)
2) `runtime_decode_steps` or `generated_tokens` (so decode_ms / generated_token)
3) `scheduler_batch_size` (distribution)
4) `scheduler_queue_ms` (already adding)

Noah Patel: and a derived panel: prefill% = prefill_ms / (prefill_ms+decode_ms)

Hiro Tanaka: careful w/ per-request histograms + per-model labels. we already got burned on cardinality.

Hiro Tanaka: can we do aggregated counters and a couple histograms with fixed buckets? ex: decode_ms_per_token histogram.

Logan Wright: re cardinality: if we do label set {model_id, model_version, region, sku, runtime_build_id} it’s already borderline.
Logan Wright: pls do NOT add prompt_len_bucket as a label if we can avoid; do bucketing in the canary config and emit separate metric names? (still not great) or just keep it as an exemplar field in logs/traces.

Nadia Rahman: +1. also note that model_version is required for baseline stability, but runtime_build_id is high churn. for dashboards/alerts we probably want runtime_build_id only in logs/trace links, not in metric labels.

Noah Patel: makes sense. canary alerts need to point to “what changed” though.

Caleb Johnson: suggestion: keep metrics low-card (model_id, region, sku). then attach runtime_build_id + kernel_id via trace attributes / structured logs, and add links from Grafana panel -> trace query.

Caleb Johnson: we can also add a metric “current runtime_build_id” as a gauge with same low-card labels (value encoded as label is bad). Better is a dashboard variable from deployment metadata.

Vanessa Ortiz: from fleet side, sku is necessary (A100 vs H100 behavior is different) and we’ve seen canary variance when the pool mix shifts.
Vanessa Ortiz: region+sku is fine; pls don’t add node_id.

Rina Kobayashi: for canary workload we already track prompt/gen lengths in the harness. we can emit “requested prefill tokens” + “requested decode tokens” as metrics (no customer content), would help normalize.

Hiro Tanaka: kernel attribution: can we get a stable `kernel_family` or `attention_impl` tag? exact kernel build hash might be too granular.

Omar El-Sayed: we added kernel selection identifiers in the runtime (the `kernel_id` + `attention_backend`). we can surface `attention_backend` as low-card label (flashv2 vs xformers vs triton) and keep `kernel_id` in traces.

Sofia Ivanova: if we can get “effective batch size during decode” that’s huge. the scheduler can have batch=32 at prefill but decode effectively 4 bc of early EOS / stream pacing.

Omar El-Sayed: we can export:
- `scheduler_active_sequences` (gauge)
- `scheduler_decode_batch_size` (histogram)
- `scheduler_prefill_batch_size` (histogram)

Omar El-Sayed: but note: histograms are expensive; we need to be careful w/ retention + bucket count.

Logan Wright: pls run through the observability cost check. if we add 3 histograms * models * regions it’s still ok, but bucket count must be small (like 8-12 buckets max).

Noah Patel: proposed metric names (align with existing canary ones):
- `ri_canary_runtime_prefill_ms` (hist)
- `ri_canary_runtime_decode_ms` (hist)
- `ri_canary_runtime_decode_ms_per_token` (hist)
- `ri_canary_scheduler_queue_ms` (hist) [already]
- `ri_canary_scheduler_decode_batch_size` (hist)
- `ri_canary_generated_tokens_total` (counter)
- `ri_canary_prefill_tokens_total` (counter)

Noah Patel: labels: `model_id`, `region`, `hardware_sku`, `tier` (hosted/dedicated?)

Nadia Rahman: i’d avoid `tier` as label unless we truly need it for alerting. it doubles series and we can infer from endpoint/env in some cases.

Sofia Ivanova: for RCA tier matters sometimes (dedicated has different admission control), but maybe keep it config-level (separate canary job per tier) and keep labels same.

Caleb Johnson: also for split: do we want end-to-end prefill/decode (includes queueing) vs runtime-only? please keep runtime-only separate so we don’t double count.

Noah Patel: agree. E2E stays `ri_canary_latency_ms`. runtime-only ones are the new ones.

Hiro Tanaka: buckets suggestion:
- prefill_ms: [5, 10, 20, 40, 80, 160, 320, +Inf]
- decode_ms_per_token: [0.2, 0.4, 0.8, 1.6, 3.2, 6.4, +Inf] (ms/token)
- batch_size: [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64, +Inf]

Omar El-Sayed: those buckets seem reasonable.

Vanessa Ortiz: + include a panel for “batch_size median + p10” so we see when batching collapses due to admission control.

Logan Wright: action items:
1) Omar to add runtime exports for prefill/decode split + batch sizes (or confirm existing metrics names)
2) Noah/Rina update canary emitter to include tokens counters + ms-per-token derived metric
3) Nadia to update baseline schema so we can store baseline for decode_ms_per_token + batch_size distribution (or at least p50/p95)

Noah Patel: i’ll open a small follow-up in ENG-4872 for “additional attribution metrics” and tag you all.

Sofia Ivanova: + can we add a runbook note: “if decode_ms_per_token regresses but batch_size stable => likely kernel/runtime; if batch_size drops but decode_ms_per_token stable => scheduler/admission/fleet”? would help oncall.

Hiro Tanaka: yes please. that decision tree is exactly what we want.

Omar El-Sayed: i can get a draft PR up today for scheduler metrics. but i’m going to keep labels minimal: model_id, region, sku. no per-route.

Logan Wright: :thumbsup: (and pls ping me for metric review before merge)

Noah Patel: :thumbsup: thanks all. goal is to make the next incident thread shorter than 200 messages 😅

Caleb Johnson: lol. also pls include trace link in alert payload so we can jump to an example slow request and see kernel_id + backend.

Noah Patel: yep, will coordinate w/ incident-bot format. end.
text:
Noah Patel: want to propose we add a couple attribution metrics to perf-canary + runtime telemetry. right now when we see p95 up or tok/s down we’re still guessing if it’s prefill vs decode vs scheduler vs batching.

Noah Patel: specifically:
- prefill vs decode time split (runtime-only)
- batch depth / effective batch size during decode
- maybe “tokens per request” + “prefill tokens” so we can normalize shapes

Sofia Ivanova: +1. queueing_time helps, but last incident we couldn’t tell if tok/s drop was due to smaller batches or slower kernel.

Sofia Ivanova: for batching depth, can we expose something like: avg_active_sequences, avg_batch_size, and distribution? tail matters.

Omar El-Sayed: we have some of this internally in scheduler debug counters, but not exported in a stable way.

Omar El-Sayed: what’s the minimum set you want on the canary side vs globally in runtime metrics?

Noah Patel: for canary RCA I’d take:
1) `runtime_prefill_ms` and `runtime_decode_ms` (per request)
2) `runtime_decode_steps` or `generated_tokens` (so decode_ms / generated_token)
3) `scheduler_batch_size` (distribution)
4) `scheduler_queue_ms` (already adding)

Noah Patel: and a derived panel: prefill% = prefill_ms / (prefill_ms+decode_ms)

Hiro Tanaka: careful w/ per-request histograms + per-model labels. we already got burned on cardinality.

Hiro Tanaka: can we do aggregated counters and a couple histograms with fixed buckets? ex: decode_ms_per_token histogram.

Logan Wright: re cardinality: if we do label set {model_id, model_version, region, sku, runtime_build_id} it’s already borderline.
Logan Wright: pls do NOT add prompt_len_bucket as a label if we can avoid; do bucketing in the canary config and emit separate metric names? (still not great) or just keep it as an exemplar field in logs/traces.

Nadia Rahman: +1. also note that model_version is required for baseline stability, but runtime_build_id is high churn. for dashboards/alerts we probably want runtime_build_id only in logs/trace links, not in metric labels.

Noah Patel: makes sense. canary alerts need to point to “what changed” though.

Caleb Johnson: suggestion: keep metrics low-card (model_id, region, sku). then attach runtime_build_id + kernel_id via trace attributes / structured logs, and add links from Grafana panel -> trace query.

Caleb Johnson: we can also add a metric “current runtime_build_id” as a gauge with same low-card labels (value encoded as label is bad). Better is a dashboard variable from deployment metadata.

Vanessa Ortiz: from fleet side, sku is necessary (A100 vs H100 behavior is different) and we’ve seen canary variance when the pool mix shifts.
Vanessa Ortiz: region+sku is fine; pls don’t add node_id.

Rina Kobayashi: for canary workload we already track prompt/gen lengths in the harness. we can emit “requested prefill tokens” + “requested decode tokens” as metrics (no customer content), would help normalize.

Hiro Tanaka: kernel attribution: can we get a stable `kernel_family` or `attention_impl` tag? exact kernel build hash might be too granular.

Omar El-Sayed: we added kernel selection identifiers in the runtime (the `kernel_id` + `attention_backend`). we can surface `attention_backend` as low-card label (flashv2 vs xformers vs triton) and keep `kernel_id` in traces.

Sofia Ivanova: if we can get “effective batch size during decode” that’s huge. the scheduler can have batch=32 at prefill but decode effectively 4 bc of early EOS / stream pacing.

Omar El-Sayed: we can export:
- `scheduler_active_sequences` (gauge)
- `scheduler_decode_batch_size` (histogram)
- `scheduler_prefill_batch_size` (histogram)

Omar El-Sayed: but note: histograms are expensive; we need to be careful w/ retention + bucket count.

Logan Wright: pls run through the observability cost check. if we add 3 histograms * models * regions it’s still ok, but bucket count must be small (like 8-12 buckets max).

Noah Patel: proposed metric names (align with existing canary ones):
- `ri_canary_runtime_prefill_ms` (hist)
- `ri_canary_runtime_decode_ms` (hist)
- `ri_canary_runtime_decode_ms_per_token` (hist)
- `ri_canary_scheduler_queue_ms` (hist) [already]
- `ri_canary_scheduler_decode_batch_size` (hist)
- `ri_canary_generated_tokens_total` (counter)
- `ri_canary_prefill_tokens_total` (counter)

Noah Patel: labels: `model_id`, `region`, `hardware_sku`, `tier` (hosted/dedicated?)

Nadia Rahman: i’d avoid `tier` as label unless we truly need it for alerting. it doubles series and we can infer from endpoint/env in some cases.

Sofia Ivanova: for RCA tier matters sometimes (dedicated has different admission control), but maybe keep it config-level (separate canary job per tier) and keep labels same.

Caleb Johnson: also for split: do we want end-to-end prefill/decode (includes queueing) vs runtime-only? please keep runtime-only separate so we don’t double count.

Noah Patel: agree. E2E stays `ri_canary_latency_ms`. runtime-only ones are the new ones.

Hiro Tanaka: buckets suggestion:
- prefill_ms: [5, 10, 20, 40, 80, 160, 320, +Inf]
- decode_ms_per_token: [0.2, 0.4, 0.8, 1.6, 3.2, 6.4, +Inf] (ms/token)
- batch_size: [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64, +Inf]

Omar El-Sayed: those buckets seem reasonable.

Vanessa Ortiz: + include a panel for “batch_size median + p10” so we see when batching collapses due to admission control.

Logan Wright: action items:
1) Omar to add runtime exports for prefill/decode split + batch sizes (or confirm existing metrics names)
2) Noah/Rina update canary emitter to include tokens counters + ms-per-token derived metric
3) Nadia to update baseline schema so we can store baseline for decode_ms_per_token + batch_size distribution (or at least p50/p95)

Noah Patel: i’ll open a small follow-up in ENG-4872 for “additional attribution metrics” and tag you all.

Sofia Ivanova: + can we add a runbook note: “if decode_ms_per_token regresses but batch_size stable => likely kernel/runtime; if batch_size drops but decode_ms_per_token stable => scheduler/admission/fleet”? would help oncall.

Hiro Tanaka: yes please. that decision tree is exactly what we want.

Omar El-Sayed: i can get a draft PR up today for scheduler metrics. but i’m going to keep labels minimal: model_id, region, sku. no per-route.

Logan Wright: :thumbsup: (and pls ping me for metric review before merge)

Noah Patel: :thumbsup: thanks all. goal is to make the next incident thread shorter than 200 messages 😅

Caleb Johnson: lol. also pls include trace link in alert payload so we can jump to an example slow request and see kernel_id + backend.

Noah Patel: yep, will coordinate w/ incident-bot format. end.

title: #eng-runtime: 1770805606
preview:
Noah Patel: trying to reconcile a thing from last night: microbench suite shows ~8-10% tokens/sec drop on llama-3.1-70b (H100) after the kernel change, but perf-canary e2e is basically flat (maybe +2% p95, within noise). are we just measuring different worlds or missing a signal?

Hiro Tanaka: different worlds mostly. microbench is single-stream, no scheduler contention, fixed seq lens. canary is going through full stack (queue + batch + cache), and our scheduler changes can hide kernel slowdowns by shifting batch composition.

Sofia Ivanova: +1. e2e includes queueing + continuous batching effects. also: microbench uses decode-heavy (1 token prefill, long decode) in some tests. canary prompt distro is more mixed. a kernel regression might be isolated to specific (prefill, seq_len) combos.

Rina Kobayashi: fwiw the microbench “decode_2048” case is the one that regressed hardest. our canary buckets don’t go that long right now (we cap at 512 decode in v0). might be why.

Noah Patel: ok, but the worry is: kernel regression *exists* and we won’t see it until some customer workload hits that shape.

Logan Wright: from telemetry side: the canary metrics we have are too “coarse” to tie back to microbench results. we alert on e2e + runtime-only histogram, but runtime-only is still lumped (prefill+decode).

Omar El-Sayed: yup. we need stage-level counters/histograms out of runtime (prefill vs decode) + maybe attention kernel time vs “other”. right now attribution is queueing vs execution only.

Nadia Rahman: also careful w labels/cardinality. if we add “test_case” or “shape_id” per request we’ll blow up quickly.

Noah Patel: can we add a *small* set of extra counters that allow us to say “microbench regression should correlate with X”? not per-prompt-id, but like bucketed lens.

Hiro Tanaka: proposed minimal additions:
- `decode_token_time_ms` summary/histogram (or `decode_tokens_per_sec` derived) bucketed by model + runtime_build + kernel_id
- `prefill_time_ms` histogram bucketed by prompt_len_bucket
- `attn_kernel_variant` tag (we already have kernel selection id but it’s not in the canary payload today)

Sofia Ivanova: and scheduler-side:
- `effective_batch_size` distribution split by stage (prefill/decode)
- `queue_wait_ms` already exists but we should show it next to runtime execution in the alert payload

Noah Patel: microbench vs e2e framing:
1) microbench is “pure kernel/runtime capacity” under controlled load
2) e2e canary is “customer-ish experience” (scheduler + batching + queue)
=> regressions can show up in one but not the other.

Rina Kobayashi: can we align at least one canary shape to the microbench worst-case? e.g. add a “long decode” bucket at low QPS so it doesn’t eat budget.

Noah Patel: i like that. add a tiny % of traffic to “sentinel shapes” that are known risk points.

Nadia Rahman: if we do that, PLEASE keep the label set stable. we can add a boolean like `workload_class=main|sentinel` not an arbitrary name.

Logan Wright: +1. `workload_class` as enum is fine. don’t do free-form.

Omar El-Sayed: for counters, what about something like:
```text
runtime_stage_latency_ms{stage="prefill", prompt_len_bucket="0-128", workload_class="main"}
runtime_stage_latency_ms{stage="decode", decode_len_bucket="513-2048", workload_class="sentinel"}
```
then we can compute per-bucket p95 and compare to baseline.

Hiro Tanaka: and add `kernel_id` as a tag *only on the canary service metrics*, not on per-request app metrics. cardinality is bounded by kernel variants (~tens).

Sofia Ivanova: plus we should explicitly track “tokens/sec at fixed concurrency” somewhere. e2e tokens/sec is affected by scheduler throughput. microbench is fixed concurrency.

Noah Patel: canary already controls its own QPS but not concurrency (it gets what it gets based on batching). can we add an internal mode that runs N workers with fixed outstanding requests? (bounded, low budget)

Rina Kobayashi: microbench harness already does fixed concurrency. but running that in prod is scary.

Omar El-Sayed: we can approximate: canary spawns `N` goroutines per model and keeps `N` in-flight. still hits production scheduler but keeps offered load consistent.

Nadia Rahman: if we add that, baseline needs to store “mode” as dimension or we’ll compare apples/oranges.

Noah Patel: ok, action items:
- @Omar can you spec stage-level latency histograms (prefill/decode) w bounded buckets + workload_class enum
- @Hiro add kernel_id into canary metric tags + ensure it lines up w runtime kernel-selection tag
- @Rina propose 1-2 sentinel shapes (long decode, long prefill) that map to microbench cases, verify budget impact
- @Logan/@Nadia sanity-check label set + retention cost

Hiro Tanaka: :thumbsup: on kernel_id. we already have `runtime.kernel_selection_id` but not all paths set it (some fall back). i’ll make sure we always emit a default like `unknown` so alerts don’t drop series.

Sofia Ivanova: one more thought: for e2e vs microbench mismatch, we should surface “effective achieved occupancy” signals (SM occupancy is too deep), but we can use proxy: `gpu_time_ms / wall_time_ms` from runtime if we have it.

Omar El-Sayed: we don’t have GPU time reliably across vendors. but we can expose “active decoding steps” vs “idle” as scheduler counters. basically: time spent waiting for batch fill vs time executing.

Noah Patel: yep, that helps explain “kernel slower but scheduler hides it by filling bigger batches” vs “kernel slower and everything tanks”.

Logan Wright: please make sure any new histograms are limited. our ingestion cost jumped last time we added wide-bucket histos.

Nadia Rahman: +1. prefer a small bucket set (maybe 6-8). and keep `prompt_len_bucket` and `decode_len_bucket` as fixed enums.

Rina Kobayashi: sentinel proposal (tentative):
- sentinel A: prefill 2k tokens, decode 128
- sentinel B: prefill 128, decode 2k
both at low frequency (1-2% of canary requests)

Noah Patel: works. let’s also update the “microbench vs e2e” section in design doc / runbook so oncall doesn’t panic if they diverge.

Sofia Ivanova: :+1: canary alert payload should include which buckets regressed. “p95 +10%” without stage/bucket info is hard to triage.

Hiro Tanaka: agreed. if we can point at “decode>512 bucket regressed w kernel_id=attn_v7”, it’s instantly actionable.

Noah Patel: cool. i’ll create a follow-up ticket for the additional counters + sentinel shapes. thanks all.
